agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"POST /validate","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"No circuit breaker protecting the service from SmartyStreets outages. A sustained SmartyStreets failure will exhaust threads/connections as requests pile up waiting for the 3s timeout."},{"from":"/health","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"Health check couples liveness to an external dependency. If SmartyStreets is down, orchestrators (k8s) will kill healthy pods, causing a cascading outage of the validation service itself. Liveness and readiness probes should be separated."}],"contract_compliance":"No contract validation observed. The service does not log or validate the shape of the SmartyStreets response. A schema change from the vendor would produce silent incorrect results.","data_flow_integrity":"Cannot verify data flow integrity at 3am — input address and raw API response are not logged, so there is no way to reproduce or diagnose a misvalidation after the fact."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No request ID / correlation ID in any log line — impossible to trace a single request across logs","No input address logged — cannot reproduce issues reported by callers","No raw SmartyStreets response logged (even at DEBUG level) — cannot diagnose vendor-side changes","No duration logged for the external API call — cannot detect latency degradation from logs alone","No structured logging apparent — grep-based debugging will be fragile"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"minimal — single counter with no dimensional breakdown","gaps":["validation_requests_total lacks a result label (valid/invalid/error) — cannot distinguish success from failure volume","No latency histogram (e.g. validation_duration_seconds) — cannot set SLO-based alerts or detect p99 degradation","No error counter or error-rate metric for SmartyStreets call failures","No in-flight request gauge — cannot detect thread/connection pool exhaustion","No metric for SmartyStreets timeout rate"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed trace context propagation — if callers pass a trace ID, it is dropped","No span for the SmartyStreets outbound call — invisible in any trace view"]},"debugging_capability":"Poor. An on-call engineer at 3am receiving a report of 'addresses coming back invalid that should be valid' has no request ID to search for, no input to compare, no vendor response to inspect, and no latency data to correlate. They would be flying blind."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"SmartyStreets returns 5xx or is unreachable","handling":"partial","recommendation":"3s timeout exists which is good, but there is no circuit breaker. Under sustained failure, every inbound request will block for 3s before failing. Add a circuit breaker (e.g. 50% error rate over 10s window → open for 30s) and return a clear 503 with a machine-readable error code."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets latency degrades (responds in 2.5s instead of 200ms)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No latency histogram means this is invisible until callers start timing out. Add a histogram metric and alert on p99 > 1s."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets rate-limits the service (HTTP 429)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No differentiation between 429 and other errors. Should implement backoff/retry for 429s specifically, and expose a metric for rate-limit hits."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets returns 200 with an unexpected/changed response schema","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Validate the response schema. Log and metric on parse failures. Return a 502 rather than silently returning 'invalid'."},{"scenario":"Upstream sends malformed or empty address payload","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No mention of input validation. Should reject bad input with 400 before calling SmartyStreets, and log the rejection."},{"scenario":"Pod killed during in-flight request due to coupled health check","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Separate liveness probe (always 200 if process is up) from readiness probe (depends on SmartyStreets reachability). This prevents orchestrator from killing pods during a vendor outage."}],"retry_config":"None observed. A single transient network blip results in a failed validation. Recommend 1 retry with jitter for 5xx/timeout, no retry for 4xx.","timeout_config":"3s timeout configured — reasonable for a synchronous external call. However, consider whether callers have their own timeout that is shorter than 3s, which would make this ineffective.","circuit_breakers":"None. This is a must-have before production given the hard dependency on an external vendor API.","graceful_degradation":"None. When SmartyStreets is unavailable, the service has no fallback. Consider: (1) returning a 'could not validate' status distinct from 'invalid' so callers can decide to accept unvalidated addresses, or (2) caching recent validations for replay."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous container image. Acceptable for a stateless service with no database migrations. Verify image tag is pinned and available in registry.","monitoring_coverage":"Insufficient. A single undifferentiated counter cannot power any meaningful alert. You cannot alert on error rate, latency degradation, or SmartyStreets availability from current metrics.","on_call_documentation":"Not mentioned. At minimum, a runbook should cover: (1) what to do when SmartyStreets is down, (2) how to verify the service is working end-to-end, (3) how to read the logs, (4) escalation contacts at SmartyStreets.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add circuit breaker configuration (error threshold, open duration, half-open probe count)","Add retry policy configuration (max retries, backoff base, jitter)"],"infra_provisioning":["Prometheus alerting rules for error rate and latency","Grafana dashboard or equivalent for the service"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"Stateless service, no database involved."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Logging and metrics are insufficient for production operation","reason":"No request IDs, no input/output logging, no latency histogram, no error-rate metric, no distributed tracing. An on-call engineer cannot diagnose any issue without these. The single counter metric cannot power any meaningful alert. This service is unmonitorable in its current state.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":55,"high_confidence_areas":["Resilience gaps (no circuit breaker, no retry) are clear from the described architecture","Observability gaps are concrete and enumerable","Rollback strategy is sound for a stateless service"],"low_confidence_areas":["Cannot verify actual code quality, error handling paths, or configuration without reading source","Unknown whether callers handle a 'validation unavailable' scenario gracefully","Unknown SmartyStreets API rate limits and current usage relative to those limits","Unknown deployment topology (single region? multi-region? how many pods?)"],"assumptions":["Service is stateless with no database","SmartyStreets is the sole external dependency","Container orchestration (k8s or similar) is the deployment target","No existing alerting rules are configured for this service"]}}
